perturbation.original_text: HSWI shall maintain complete and accurate records to support and document the Royalties owed under this Agreement in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied and to document compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: HSWI shall endeavor to maintain records to support and document the Royalties owed under this Agreement in a manner it deems reasonably consistent with accounting principles and to generally document compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates 'complete and accurate records' maintained in accordance with GAAP, while the modified text introduces ambiguity by stating HSWI 'shall endeavor to maintain records' in a manner it deems 'reasonably consistent' with accounting principles. The original clause requires explicit documentation, while the modified clause allows for subjective interpretation.
perturbation.law_citation: 15 U.S. Code § 7262 - Improper influence on conduct of audits
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 7262 of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act emphasizes the accuracy and reliability of financial records and audits. By allowing HSWI to maintain records in a manner it deems 'reasonably consistent' with accounting principles, the modified text potentially undermines the integrity of financial reporting and conflicts with the SOX requirement for accurate and reliable financial data used for audits and investor protection.

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the Sarbanes-Oxley Act's emphasis on accurate and reliable financial records. By changing 'shall maintain complete and accurate records' to 'shall endeavor to maintain records in a manner it deems reasonably consistent with accounting principles,' the modified text introduces ambiguity and subjectivity, potentially undermining the integrity of financial reporting. Snippet 1, referencing 15 USC 7262, emphasizes the 'responsibility of management for establishing and maintaining an adequate internal control structure,' which aligns with the original text's requirement for complete and accurate records. The changed text weakens this requirement, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party shall maintain all of the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party in strict confidence and will protect such information with the same degree of care that each party exercises with its own Confidential Information, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party shall try to maintain the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party in confidence and will protect such information with a degree of care it finds appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates 'strict confidence' and a 'reasonable degree of care,' setting a clear standard. The modified text weakens this by stating 'try to maintain...in confidence' and protect with a degree of care it 'finds appropriate,' introducing subjectivity and ambiguity. This makes the obligation much less enforceable.
perturbation.law_citation: 18 U.S. Code § 1832 - Theft of trade secrets
perturbation.law_explanation: The Economic Espionage Act criminalizes the theft of trade secrets. While this contract section concerns *confidential* information (which may or may not rise to the level of 'trade secret'), the change introduces ambiguity such that the level of protection may be insufficient to prevent trade secret theft. The original clause with its more stringent requirements, better aligns with the intent to prevent information from being misappropriated. The 'degree of care it finds appropriate' language could be interpreted to mean a very low level of care, failing to protect potentially valuable confidential information and possibly rising to the level of negligence.

perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the 'strict confidence' and 'reasonable degree of care' standards with a subjective 'try to maintain...in confidence' and 'degree of care it finds appropriate'. This weakens the obligation significantly. The Law Explanation argues that this introduces ambiguity that could lead to insufficient protection of trade secrets, potentially rising to negligence. While the scraped snippets primarily address the Economic Espionage Act and theft of trade secrets (18 USC 1832), the principle of protecting sensitive information is relevant. The original clause better aligns with preventing misappropriation, and the modified language could be interpreted as a very low level of care, contradicting the intent to protect confidential information, even if it does not meet the high bar of a 'trade secret'. The modification undermines the original intent to ensure protection, creating a potentially unenforceable obligation.

perturbation.original_text: World Book hereby represents and warrants that: (i) it has all applicable rights in and to the Content and the Content does not and will not infringe or misappropriate any Intellectual Property Rights of any third persons; (ii) it will perform all services in a professional, diligent, and workmanlike manner; (iii) the Content will be free from material errors or inaccuracies; (iv) the Content will comply with all laws and regulations of the People's Republic of China, including without limitation for distribution, publication and educational use as contemplated herein; and, (v) the Content as delivered by World Book is expressly approved by or within the applicable guidelines of the national government, any ministries, and provincial governments, as applicable, of the People's Republic of China for distribution, publication and education use as contemplated herein.
perturbation.changed_text: World Book hereby represents and warrants that: (i) to the best of its current understanding, it has rights in and to the Content, and it has no current knowledge that the Content infringes or misappropriates any Intellectual Property Rights of any third persons; (ii) it will try to perform all services in a professional manner; (iii) the Content should be reasonably free from material errors or inaccuracies; (iv) World Book anticipates that the content is likely to align with the regulations of the People's Republic of China; and, (v) World Book hopes that the content is delivered such that it would likely be approved by the Chinese government.
perturbation.explanation: The original text presents explicit warranties regarding intellectual property rights, service performance, content accuracy, and regulatory compliance. The modified text weakens these guarantees with phrases like 'to the best of its current understanding,' 'no current knowledge,' 'try to perform,' 'reasonably free,' 'likely to align,' and 'hopes that...likely be approved.' These changes introduce ambiguity and reduce the enforceability of World Book's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 8 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 8 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China requires parties to abide by the principle of good faith in performing their contractual obligations. The original warranties, while beneficial to HSWI, create a clear obligation that aligns with this principle of good faith. The modified text, with its ambiguity and weakened guarantees, may be interpreted as a failure to act in good faith, since World Book is making numerous caveats, that make it much more difficult for the company to be held responsible for the quality of their content. If HSWI relies on these representations and suffers damages due to inaccuracies or regulatory non-compliance, it could be argued that World Book breached its duty of good faith under Chinese contract law. These 'weasel words' introduce doubt as to whether World Book can be considered to have been operating in good faith.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 行国有化和征收；在特殊情况下，根据社会公共利益的需要，对合营企业可以依照法律程序实行征收，并给予相应的补偿。” II. Article 3 is revised as the following:" The joint venture agreement, contract and articles of association signed by the parties to the venture shall be submitted to the state competent authorities of foreign economic relations and trade(hereafter referred to as approval authorities), and the approval authorities shall, within three months, decide whether to approve or disapprove them. After approval, the joint venture shall register with the state competent authorities of administration for industry and commerce to obtain a licence to do business and start operations." 二、 第三条修改为：“合营各方签订的合营协议、合同、章程，应报国家对外经济贸易主管部门（以下称审查批准机关）审查批准。审查批准机关应在三个月内决定批准或不批准。合营企业经批准后，向国家工商行政管理主管部门登记，领取营业执照，开始营业。” III. The first paragraph of Article 6 is revised as the following: "A joint venture shall have a board of directors, which shall have its size and composition stipulated in the contract and the articles of association after consultation between the parties to the venture, and the directors shall be appointed and replaced by the parties to the venture. The Chairman and the vice-chairman are determined by the parties to the venture or elected by the board of directors. Either party of the Chinese-foreign joint ventures may be the chairman and the other shall assume the office of vice-chairman. In handling major problems, the board of directors shall reach a decision through consultation by the parties to the venture, in accordance with the principle of equality and mutual benefit." 三、 第六条第一款修改为：“合营企业设董事会，其人数组成由合营各方协商，在合同、章程中确定，并由合营各方委派和撤换。董事长和副董事长由合营各方协商确定或由董事会选举产生。中外合营者的一方担任董事长的，由他方担任副董事长。董事会根据平等互利的原则，决定合营企业的重大问题。” IV. The Second paragraph of Article 7 is revised as the following: "A joint venture may enjoy the preferential treatment of reduction of or exemption from tax pursuant to relevant state taxation laws or administrative decrees." 四、 第七条第二款修改为：“合营企业依照国家有关税收的法律和行政法规的规定，可以享受减税、免税的优惠待遇。” V. The first paragraph of Article 8 is revised as the following: "A joint venture shall, with its business licence, open a foreign exchange account at the banks or other financial organizations approved by the state foreign exchange control administrative organs to handle foreign exchange business." 五、 第八条第一款修改为：“合营企业应凭营业执照在国家外汇管理机关允许经营外汇业务的银行或其他金融机构开立外汇帐户。” The first paragraph of Article 10 is revised as the following: " The net profit that a foreign joint venture receives after fulfilling its obligations under the laws and the agreement and the contract, the funds it receives at the time of the joint venture's scheduled expiration or early termination, and its other funds may be remitted abroad in accordance with the foreign exchange regulations and in the currency specified in the joint venture contract." 第十条第一款修改为：“外国合营者在履行法律和协议、合同规定的义务后分得的净利润，在合营企业期满或者中止时所分得的资金以及其它资金，可按合营企业合同规定的货币，按外汇管理条例汇往国外。” Article 11 is revised as the following: "The wages, salaries and other legitimate income earned by the foreign staff and workers of a joint venture, after payment of the individual income tax under the tax laws of the People's Republic of China, may be remitted abroad in accordance with the foreign exchange regulations." 第十一条修改为：“合营企业的外籍职工的工资收入和其它正当收入，按中华人民共和国税法缴纳个人所得税后，可按外汇管理条例汇往国外。” VI. Article 12 is revised as the following: "The contract period of a joint venture may be decided differently according to its particular line of business and circumstance. The joint ventures of some trades should decide the contract period; and others may or may not decide the contract period. A joint venture that has set a contract period should, if the parties to the joint venture agree to extent the contract period, apply to the approval authorities six months ahead of the expiration of the contract period. The latter should make the decision of approval or disapproval within one month as of the date of application." 六、 第十二条修改为：“合营企业的合营期限，按不同行业、不同情况，作不同的约定。有的行业的合营企业，应当约定合营期限；有的行业的合营企业，可以约定合营期限，也可以不约定合营期限。约定合营期限的合营企业，合营各
perturbation.justification: The original text contained warranties regarding content accuracy and regulatory compliance, which created a clear obligation. The modified text weakens these guarantees, introducing ambiguity that makes it more difficult to enforce those obligations. Article 8 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China requires parties to abide by the principle of good faith in performing their contractual obligations. The modified text may be interpreted as a failure to act in good faith. This is because World Book is making numerous caveats, that make it much more difficult for the company to be held responsible for the quality of their content.
